title: "podman image scp" changes manifest format
body:
### Issue Description

When transferring an image to and from rootful containers-storage, the manifest type changes from OCI to Docker, which causes some tools (i.e. Bootc) to break.

This also causes the digests to differ.

```
❯ podman pull quay.io/fedora/fedora:43                                        
Trying to pull quay.io/fedora/fedora:43...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob de6be2cc5821 done   | 
Copying config 14e8744f2b done   | 
Writing manifest to image destination
14e8744f2b4d0d6688e2f0601ce503343cb642bb9a1c6f7ef5f8179876e1693a

❯ podman inspect quay.io/fedora/fedora:43 --format '{{.ManifestType}}'
application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json

❯ podman image scp quay.io/fedora/fedora:43 root@localhost::
Copying blob c5e1252e4cab done   | 
Copying config 14e8744f2b done   | 
Writing manifest to image destination
[sudo] password for admin: 
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob c5e1252e4cab done   | 
Copying config 14e8744f2b done   | 
Writing manifest to image destination
Loaded image: quay.io/fedora/fedora:43
 
❯ sudo podman inspect quay.io/fedora/fedora:43 --format '{{.ManifestType}}'
application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue


### Describe the results you received

Transferring an image with an OCI manifest to rootful storage changes to a docker manifest.

### Describe the results you expected

The manifest type should remain the same.  Or alternatively this limitation should be documented in the docs.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
I checked the `scp` code and noticed it uses a combination of `podman save` and `podman load`. The issue is that the default format for `podman save` is `docker-archive`.

I see two ways to fix this:
1. Detect the image format first and explicitly set the output format for `podman save` in scp code.
2. Create a new format type for `podman save` (e.g., asImageType) and use that for the scp code.

WDYT? @mtrmac @Luap99 @mheon

- In general, workflows using `image scp` are almost certainly better off using a registry.
- I suppose the best format here is `dir:`, because that does not lose information. OTOH the long-term stability of that format is _a bit_ of an issue, we might add more features over time, and if the consumer is older, it might not be able to accept it.
- (Neither `oci-archive` nor `docker-archive:` preserve schema1 images unchanged — although that’s increasingly only of historical interest.)
- There is currently no way to do a c/storage-to-c/storage copy that preserves digest references to original compressed versions (`podman pull foo@compressedDigest; podman scp; podman inspect foo@compressedDigest` will always fail), because we don’t _have_ the compressed version any more, so `scp` can’t transfer it. (In principle, we could copy the original manifest and add a corresponding metadata entry. To an extent, that becomes a question of trust / digest integrity, but of course `root` can break that already.)

The real question here is what exactly breaks, is it just that bootc cannot deal with the docker format? AFAIK the manifest format is mostly meaningless for a local image? It only matters if you like to push them unless I am missing something?


> Detect the image format first and explicitly set the output format for podman save in scp code.

Do we want to deal with extra complexity, I have been looking a few times at the scp good and I really do not like it that much so I rather not introduce more complications.

I suppose another option instead of detecting we can expose the save --format to the caller on scp and let the user hand the format of their choice? Though arguably exposing a new option and documenting it is more complicated than a auto detection so I don't know.

Overall I really have no strong opinion on this command so I leave the details happily to others.
+1 Exposing `save --format` sounds much better than my ideas.
